# Roadmap to 1.0 — AI Software Studio

**Status:** Approved design (brainstorm output)
**Date:** 2026-05-18
**Owner:** Roni Moe
**Location note:** This spec is gitignored along with the rest of `docs/` per commit `574d59e`. It is a durable local working artifact, not a public source of truth.
**Implementation plans:** generated by the `writing-plans` skill, per milestone, into `docs/superpowers/plans/` (also gitignored).

---

## 1. Context

AI Software Studio is currently at `v0.0.1` — an architectural scaffold. Tauri + Next.js + Rust shell builds, the typed `bindings.ts` bridge is wired, seven dashboard panels render against a mock dispatcher, and the domain model (`Project`, `Task`, `EngineStatus`, `VerificationRun`) is defined. No real engine adapters, no SQLite persistence, no git worktree management, no process spawning, no verification execution.

This spec defines the path from `v0.0.1` to a polished `v1.0` public launch.

## 2. Target state

`v1.0` is a polished public release of the product described in the local product brief and spec, sharpened by two principles surfaced during this brainstorm:

- Local-first desktop app for macOS and Linux.
- Wraps Claude Code CLI and Codex CLI in a structured task workflow.
- End-to-end: task → constraints → worktree → agent run → captured evidence → verification → review → PR with evidence report.
- Distributed via Homebrew (primary) and direct download (best-effort, unsigned).
- Auto-update on Linux via Tauri's updater; macOS updates flow through `brew upgrade`.
- Sentry opt-in error reporting. No product analytics in 1.0.
- Free and open source under MIT.
- **Spec-first and test-first by default.** Every task begins with an agentic intake that produces persistent spec, plan, and test-plan artifacts. TDD discipline (red → green → refactor) is enforced via git-history verification, configurable per project.
- **Agentic, not procedural.** The app gives the agent skills, artifacts, and hooks as primitives, and lets it operate. The harness does not script every step — it provides the rails the agent runs on, and intervenes only at verification gates and pending-question pauses.

Out of scope for 1.0 (deferred to 1.1+): MCP integration, browser/screenshot verification, team sync, hosted runners, Windows support, Mac App Store, AUR/Flatpak, paid commercial features, third-party skill registry, multi-engine orchestration within a single task.

## 3. Decisions

The following decisions are load-bearing for everything below:

| # | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Target endpoint is polished 1.0 (not just personal MVP) | User goal is a real product, not a personal tool |
| 2 | Phased shippable milestones, not a flat checklist | Each release should be coherent and installable |
| 3 | Claude Code first, Codex CLI second | User's daily driver; faster path to a working loop |
| 4 | Scope = MVP "Must Have" + "Should Have" + selected V1 (GitHub PR + issue import + templates) | Without GitHub PR creation the loop is half-built |
| 5 | Distribution = Homebrew cask primary, direct download best-effort, no Apple Developer ID | Saves $99/yr; Homebrew handles macOS quarantine automatically |
| 6 | Linear milestones (not parallel tracks) | Solo developer; parallel tracks cost more in context-switching than they save |
| 7 | One carve-out from linear: CI/release pipeline scaffolded in v0.1 | Signing/packaging bugs always surface late; pay 1 day in v0.1 to save 1 week in v0.5 |
| 8 | Spec-first + test-first is a product principle, not a feature | Unattended agent runs need durable constraints to prevent drift into vibe coding |
| 9 | The harness is agentic — ships skills, artifacts, and hooks as primitives | Static prompts and forms aren't enough; the agent needs invokable, composable workflows |
| 10 | Skill packaging: plain Markdown skill bodies loaded via `@`-import in v0.2 (works identically on both engines), engine-native skill mechanism with per-engine adapters from v0.3 | v0.2 needs both engines working with the same skills; `@`-import gives that for free without committing to any engine's skill discovery format. v0.3 abstracts to per-engine native loading once we know what Codex actually supports |
| 11 | Build skills from scratch, using superpowers as a design reference | No vendoring, no copy/paste, no runtime dependency on superpowers being installed. Skills are first-class AI Software Studio components, owned and versioned by this project. Superpowers informs the shape (red→green discipline, brainstorming structure, plan-step verification gates) but the implementations are ours |
| 12 | Closed skill system in 1.0; open third-party skill registry deferred to 1.1+ | Skill registry is potentially the platform play but blows up 1.0 scope and trust model |
| 13 | Hybrid intake (deterministic form fallback + agentic interview skill) | Form alone feels procedural; pure-agentic feels heavy for trivial tasks |
| 14 | TDD enforcement is configurable per project, default hard | Need an escape hatch for prototyping; defaults should still reward discipline |
| 15 | Async question/pause mechanic lands in v0.2 with the rest of the spec/plan machinery | Without `NeedsInput` halt-and-wait, "unattended" means "hope the agent doesn't guess wrong" |

## 4. Milestone shape

| # | Tag | Theme | "Done" means |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | `v0.0.1` | **Scaffold** *(shipped)* | Architecture in place; mock dispatcher |
| 1 | `v0.1` | **Personal MVP** | End-to-end loop with Claude Code on your Mac. Deterministic intake → `task-brief.md` + generated `CLAUDE.md` in worktree. Soft TDD (prompt-only). CI pipeline scaffolded. `gh pr create` closes the loop. |
| 2 | `v0.2` | **Hardened MVP** | Both engines (Codex adds `AGENTS.md` generation). Agentic intake skill + spec + plan + test-plan artifacts. Agent-written runtime artifacts (journal, assumptions, questions, blockers). Async pause/resume on `NeedsInput`. Hard TDD verification via git history. Policy/risk, project config, artifact store, crash recovery, issue import. |
| 3 | `v0.3` | **Operational beta** | Engine-agnostic skill format with per-engine adapters. Per-project TDD config + escape hatch. Templates capture the whole artifact set. Settings UI, schema migrations, structured logging, integration tests. |
| 4 | `v0.5` | **Closed beta** | Real installers (Homebrew + GitHub Releases), Linux auto-update, onboarding flow, polish. |
| 5 | `v0.9` | **Open beta** | Sentry opt-in, perf budgets, accessibility pass, docs site. |
| 6 | `v1.0` | **Polished launch** | Bug bash, marketing site, demo video, security policy, launch. |

## 5. Milestone contents

### 5.1 `v0.1` — Personal MVP

**Outcome:** Open a real repo, fill in a structured intake that produces a persistent `task-brief.md` + a generated `CLAUDE.md` priming Claude Code, run the agent in an isolated worktree (the prompt contract requires test-first), watch streamed output, see the diff, run project verification commands, copy a PR evidence report to clipboard, and create a PR via `gh`. Working end-to-end on your Mac.

**Rust core**
- Replace in-memory mock services with real SQLite via `sqlx`, `app.db` under platform data dir. Initial schema: `projects`, `tasks`, `task_constraints`, `task_acceptance_criteria`, `verification_runs`. No migration framework yet (drop+recreate is acceptable at v0.1).
- `ProjectService`: open repo via OS file picker, validate git repo, persist.
- `EngineService`: detect `claude` binary on `PATH`, capture version, return real `EngineStatus`.
- `TaskService`: CRUD wired to SQLite; lifecycle transitions match `TaskStatus`.
- `GitService`: `git worktree add`, worktree destroy, `git status --porcelain`, `git diff --unified`.
- `ProcessRunner`: `tokio::process` with line-buffered stdout/stderr streaming over Tauri events. Plain stdio (no PTY yet).
- `ClaudeCodeAdapter`: spawn `claude` in the worktree. The prompt body is minimal — most context is delivered via the auto-loaded `CLAUDE.md` in the worktree root. The prompt body itself contains only: task ID, "read `CLAUDE.md` first," and the test-first / scope-boundary clauses.
- `TaskBriefService`: render the deterministic intake form's fields into `task-brief.md`. Persist to two locations:
  - Durable artifact store at `~/.local/share/ai-software-studio/projects/{project_id}/tasks/{task_id}/artifacts/task-brief.md`
  - Live worktree at `{worktree}/.aistudio/task-brief.md`
- `WorktreeContextService` (new — this is the agentic primer for v0.1):
  - At run start, write `{worktree}/CLAUDE.md` as a thin index that `@`-imports `.aistudio/task-brief.md`.
  - If the project already has a root `CLAUDE.md`, append a managed section between `<!-- aistudio:begin -->` / `<!-- aistudio:end -->` markers instead of overwriting.
  - Add `.aistudio/` to the worktree's `.gitignore` (so generated artifacts never enter the PR diff — reviewers see them via the PR evidence report body).
- `GitHubService`: detect `gh` binary + `gh auth status`. `git push -u origin <branch>` from worktree. `gh pr create --title --body --base` returning PR URL.
- `VerificationService`: run a configured shell command, capture stdout/stderr + exit code, persist as `VerificationRun`.

**Frontend**
- Wire all seven panels to real Tauri commands; mock dispatcher stays for `pnpm dev` only.
- Open-project flow via native file dialog.
- Structured intake wizard (the deterministic v0.1 form of "spec-first"): required fields = title, problem statement, acceptance criteria (multi-line, one per line), constraints, out-of-scope, files-to-touch hint. One step per concern, not a flat form. Output → `task-brief.md`.
- "Start" button: creates worktree, writes `CLAUDE.md` + `.aistudio/`, spawns Claude Code, switches to live view.
- Terminal panel subscribed to Tauri events for streaming output.
- Changed files panel from real `git status`.
- Diff viewer using `react-diff-view`.
- Verification panel runs configured commands, shows real pass/fail.
- Review room with all sections, "Copy PR report" → Markdown to clipboard.
- "Create PR" button in Review Room: enabled only when `gh` is authed and the worktree has commits. Shows returned PR URL.

**Infrastructure (the v0.1 carve-out)**
- GitHub Actions: lint, typecheck, test on every PR. Matrix: macOS-latest, ubuntu-latest.
- Create the `ronimoe/homebrew-tap` repo with a stub cask formula. Not populated yet — just exercising the workflow.

**Skills shipped in v0.1:** none (v0.1 uses static `CLAUDE.md` priming only). Skill mechanic lands in v0.2.

**Artifacts produced in v0.1:**
- Pre-run: `task-brief.md`, generated `CLAUDE.md` (worktree thin index)
- Post-run: terminal log, git diff, verification logs, PR evidence report Markdown

**Out of scope:** Codex, PTY, artifact store on disk for non-brief artifacts, risk labels, project config file, GitHub issue import, installer, settings UI, schema migrations, agentic intake (v0.2), `spec.md`/`plan.md` artifacts (v0.2), agent-written runtime artifacts (v0.2), hard TDD verification (v0.2), shipped skills (v0.2), async pause mechanic (v0.2).

---

### 5.2 `v0.2` — Hardened MVP

**Outcome:** Feature-complete MVP per the product spec, plus the full agentic spec-first/test-first machinery. Both engines work. The intake is itself an agent run that produces a real spec + plan + test-plan. The agent writes a journal, logs assumptions, and pauses on questions instead of guessing. TDD discipline is verified against git history before a task can move to Review Ready. The harness ships its first built-from-scratch skills.

**Rust core — engines and process**
- `CodexCliAdapter`, mirroring the Claude Code adapter shape. Writes/appends `AGENTS.md` in the worktree (Codex's equivalent of `CLAUDE.md`) using the same thin-index + `@`-import pattern.
- PTY support via `portable-pty` for interactive engines (refactor `ProcessRunner` to support PTY mode behind a flag).
- Stop/kill: SIGTERM → wait 2s → SIGKILL → persist final run status.
- Conversation: `send_message(run_id, message)` on the `EngineAdapter` trait.
- **`pause_with_pending_questions(run_id, questions)` on the `EngineAdapter` trait.** Halts the engine cleanly, transitions task to `NeedsInput`, surfaces questions to UI.
- Crash recovery: on startup, scan tasks in `Running`/`VerificationRunning`/`NeedsInput`, mark `Running` and `VerificationRunning` as `Failed` with reason, restore `NeedsInput` tasks intact (they were already paused).

**Rust core — artifacts, policy, config**
- Artifact store on filesystem matching the architecture doc's data layout. All engine runs write to disk, not just memory.
- Policy engine: parse `sensitive_paths` globs, label each changed file with `RiskLevel`. Default sensitive globs ship out of the box (`.env*`, `src/auth/**`, `prisma/migrations/**`, `infra/**`).
- `.aistudio/config.yaml` parsing; project config surfaced to UI.
- `GitHubService` extension: list open issues for a repo (`gh issue list --json`), import single issue → new draft task (title, body → description, labels → constraints hint).

**Rust core — agentic skill harness**
- Skill installation: at run start, copy the bundled skill Markdown bodies into `{worktree}/.aistudio/skills/` and update `CLAUDE.md` (or `AGENTS.md`) to `@`-import each one. v0.2 deliberately does **not** use any engine-native skill mechanism (e.g., Claude's `.claude/skills/` discovery) — skills are just instruction Markdown loaded via `@`-import, which works identically for Claude Code and Codex. The engine-native skill mechanism arrives in v0.3 when we abstract the skill format.
- Hook system:
  - `on-engine-start` → write `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`, install skills, drop artifacts into `.aistudio/`
  - `on-step-complete` (defined as "agent has explicitly invoked `aistudio:journal-checkpoint`") → no-op for the harness; the skill itself appends to `journal.md`
  - `on-engine-halt` → collect `questions.md` and `blockers.md`, transition task status accordingly
  - `on-task-complete` → invoke `aistudio:generate-evidence` to render the PR report
- File watchers on `.aistudio/journal.md`, `.aistudio/questions.md`, `.aistudio/assumptions.md`, `.aistudio/blockers.md`. Changes stream to the UI as Tauri events.

**Rust core — TDD verification**
- `TDDVerifier`: scan the worktree's git log. For each commit touching files matched by the project's `implementation_paths` glob (default: anything outside `**/*.test.*`, `tests/**`, `**/__tests__/**`), assert that an earlier commit in the same branch contains failing-test additions matching `test_paths`. Configurable.
- Block transition to Review Ready if verification fails, unless the task has an explicit override toggle set.

**Skills shipped in v0.2 (Claude-Code-native, built from scratch, modeled on superpowers patterns)**

Every skill is written from scratch as a first-class AI Software Studio component. Superpowers informs the design — the brainstorming question-at-a-time rhythm, the writing-plans verification-gate structure, the TDD red→green discipline — but no superpowers content is copied or vendored. The app has no runtime dependency on superpowers being installed; users without superpowers get the same experience as users with it.

- `aistudio:intake` — drive the spec interview. Modeled on the question-at-a-time / present-and-approve / write-design rhythm of `superpowers:brainstorming`. Output: `spec.md` + `interview-transcript.md` + (in the same pass) `implementation-plan.md`.
- `aistudio:write-plan` — standalone plan generator. Modeled on `superpowers:writing-plans` (ordered steps with explicit verification gates between them). Used when intake is skipped (e.g., issue-imported tasks).
- `aistudio:tdd-cycle` — enforce red → green → refactor for one implementation step. Modeled on `superpowers:test-driven-development`.
- `aistudio:journal-checkpoint` — append a structured entry to `journal.md`.
- `aistudio:pause-for-question` — write to `questions.md` and halt cleanly with `NeedsInput`.
- `aistudio:assumption-log` — record an assumption made on ambiguity.
- `aistudio:verify-acceptance` — run test plan + check criteria. Modeled on `superpowers:verification-before-completion`.
- `aistudio:generate-evidence` — produce the PR evidence report.

**Frontend**
- Engine picker in task creation (Claude Code | Codex CLI).
- Intake: deterministic form (from v0.1) becomes the fallback path. Default flow now triggers the `aistudio:intake` skill via a brief "interviewer" engine run that asks clarifying questions in the conversation panel. The interview transcript persists. The output spec is editable before "Start."
- Conversation panel: dual mode — async question queue (pending `questions.md` items rendered as "Agent paused — N questions") + free-form messages to a live run. Answering a question appends to `questions.md`, transitions task back to `Running`, agent resumes.
- Banner in dashboard shell when any task is in `NeedsInput` state.
- Live-tailed views of `journal.md`, `assumptions.md`, `blockers.md` in the Agent Workspace panel.
- Risk badges on changed files (`safe/sensitive/dependency/migration/infra`).
- Project config viewer (read-only; editor lands in v0.3).
- Stop button wired to Rust stop.
- Crash recovery banner on startup.
- "Import from issue" flow in the task board.
- Review Room: show spec, plan, test-plan, journal, assumptions, decisions in addition to existing diff + verification sections.

**Artifacts produced in v0.2**

| Origin | Artifact | Where |
|---|---|---|
| Intake (agentic) | `spec.md`, `interview-transcript.md`, `implementation-plan.md`, `test-plan.md`, `constraints.md`, `out-of-scope.md` | `{worktree}/.aistudio/` + durable artifact store |
| Harness | `CLAUDE.md` (Claude) or `AGENTS.md` (Codex) | `{worktree}/` root |
| Agent (runtime, via skills) | `journal.md`, `assumptions.md`, `questions.md`, `blockers.md` | `{worktree}/.aistudio/` + durable artifact store |
| Capture | terminal log, git diff snapshot, verification logs | durable artifact store |
| Skill | PR evidence report | durable artifact store + PR body |

**Out of scope:** Engine-native skill mechanism (v0.3 — v0.2 ships plain Markdown skill bodies loaded via `@`-import from `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`, which works identically for both engines), templates capturing artifact sets (v0.3), settings UI (v0.3), schema migration tooling (v0.3), installer (v0.5).

---

### 5.3 `v0.3` — Operational beta

**Outcome:** Stops being a personal tool. You'd hand-deliver builds to ~5 trusted devs and not be embarrassed by missing keyboard shortcuts, broken empty states, or schema migrations that wiped their data. Skills are engine-agnostic. TDD discipline is configurable per project.

**Rust core**
- Engine-agnostic skill format. Define our own minimal skill manifest (name, description, body, optional triggers). Per-engine adapters translate to the engine's native loading mechanism:
  - Claude: writes to `.claude/skills/` in the worktree and updates `CLAUDE.md`
  - Codex: writes to `.codex/skills/` (or whatever Codex consumes) and updates `AGENTS.md`
- Schema migration framework via `sqlx migrate`. From here on, every schema change ships a migration.
- Structured logging via `tracing` crate; log file rotation (daily, keep 7); log location exposed in settings.
- Task templates: save a task as template, instantiate from template. **A template captures the whole artifact set** (spec, plan, test-plan, constraints, out-of-scope) — not just title/description.
- Settings persistence: app-level preferences (default engine, default verification commands, data dir, sensitive paths defaults, log level).
- Background cleanup job: prune worktrees for terminal-state tasks older than N days; prune artifact logs over a size threshold.
- Telemetry instrumentation hooks (counters, durations) writing to local file only. Transmission lands in v0.9.

**TDD enforcement config (v0.3 makes it configurable)**
- Per-project in `.aistudio/config.yaml`:
  ```yaml
  tdd:
    enabled: true
    test_paths: ["**/*.test.*", "tests/**"]
    implementation_paths: ["src/**", "lib/**"]
    override_requires_comment: true
  ```
- Per-task escape hatch in UI (one-click override, must include a reason — recorded as an `assumptions.md` entry).

**Frontend**
- Settings UI: paths, default engine, default commands, sensitive-paths defaults, TDD strictness toggle, GitHub auth status with re-auth flow.
- Templates: "Save as template" on a task; template picker on the new-task flow.
- Project config: full editor for `.aistudio/config.yaml`.
- Keyboard shortcuts: ⌘N new task, ⌘, settings, ⌘K command palette (stretch).
- Empty, loading, and error states across all seven panels.

**Quality**
- Integration tests: temp git repo + mock engine binary, exercising the full loop end-to-end. At least one per engine.
- Component tests for Review Room and Changed Files.
- Snapshot test for PR report Markdown generation.
- Test that confirms `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md` generation correctly preserves an existing project-level file.

**Docs**
- README "How to use it" section with screenshots.
- CHANGELOG entries for everything since `v0.0.1`.

**Out of scope:** Real installers, signing, auto-update, Sentry, perf budgets, accessibility pass, docs site, third-party skill registry.

---

### 5.4 `v0.5` — Closed beta

**Outcome:** A friend with a download link can install and use it without your help. Linux auto-updates work. The thing looks like a real app, not `pnpm tauri:dev`.

**Distribution**
- macOS: unsigned `.app` from GitHub Releases. README documents the `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine` workaround.
- macOS: Homebrew cask in `ronimoe/homebrew-tap`, populated for real. `brew install ronimoe/tap/ai-software-studio` works end-to-end. Cask formula runs the xattr step automatically.
- Linux: `.AppImage` and `.deb` builds.
- GitHub Actions: on `v*` tag push, build all three targets, generate Tauri update manifest, upload to GitHub Releases, auto-bump the cask formula in `homebrew-tap` repo via a separate workflow.
- Tauri auto-updater: enabled on Linux (`.AppImage`); disabled on macOS (Homebrew handles updates via `brew upgrade`). In-app "update available" banner on macOS deeplinks to the `brew upgrade` command.

**Onboarding**
- First-run welcome screen.
- Engine detection wizard: checks `claude`, `gh`, `git`. For each missing one, shows install instructions (Homebrew command, official docs link).
- Sample task tutorial: ships with a "Try it" sample repo flow walking through one full task end-to-end — including the intake interview and a paused-for-question moment to demo the unattended-friendly behavior.
- "About" dialog: version, update check, license, link to docs.

**Polish**
- Real app icon (commission or design).
- Native macOS menu bar (File/Edit/View/Window/Help) with shortcuts wired to in-app actions.
- Window state persistence (size, position, sidebar widths).
- Single-instance lock (prevent two copies pointed at the same data dir).
- Graceful shutdown: persist pending state on quit, recover on relaunch.

**Out of scope:** Apple Developer ID enrollment, notarization, MAS, Sentry transmission, perf budgets, accessibility pass, docs site.

---

### 5.5 `v0.9` — Open beta

**Outcome:** Anyone landing on the README can install and use it. You'd link to it on Twitter without cringing. Perf is budgeted, errors get reported with consent, it's accessible.

**Telemetry & error reporting**
- Sentry in Rust core + frontend.
- Opt-in consent screen on first run. Default: off. Clear about what's sent (error types, stack traces, app version, OS) and what's never sent (repo content, diffs, terminal output, task descriptions, engine prompts, **`spec.md`/`plan.md`/`journal.md` contents**).
- Settings toggle to enable/disable mid-flight.
- Local crash report viewer ("here's exactly what would have been sent") for trust.

**Performance budgets (enforced in CI where possible)**
- Cold start to interactive: < 2s on M-series Mac, < 4s on a five-year-old Linux laptop.
- Worktree creation + skill install: < 500ms for a typical repo.
- Large diff handling: virtualized renderer; no UI freeze on diffs > 10MB.
- Engine output streaming: holds 60fps with > 1000 lines/sec.
- File-watcher latency for `journal.md`/`questions.md`: UI updates within 250ms of disk write.
- SQLite UI-blocking queries: < 50ms.
- Bundle size: < 50MB for the desktop binary.

**Accessibility**
- Keyboard navigation pass (every interactive element tab-reachable, escape closes overlays).
- Visible focus indicators in both themes.
- ARIA labels on critical UI (status badges, actions, panels, pending-question banner).
- WCAG AA contrast audit.
- `prefers-reduced-motion` respected.

**Docs site**
- Real docs site using Astro Starlight.
- Hosted at `docs.aisoftware.studio` (or GitHub Pages fallback).
- Pages: Getting Started, Task Workflow, Engine Setup, **Skills Reference** (catalog of bundled skills + when each fires), **Artifact Reference** (what each artifact is, who writes it, how to use it), Settings Reference, Project Config Reference, Troubleshooting, FAQ.
- README links to docs site as canonical reference; in-repo `docs/` stays as the local dev/architecture reference (still gitignored).

**Testing**
- Integration suite running real `claude` and `gh` binaries in CI (cached, gated by environment so they can be skipped locally).
- E2E tests via Tauri webdriver or Playwright pointed at `pnpm dev`.
- Visual regression on dashboard and review room screens.

**Out of scope:** Apple Developer ID, MAS, AUR/Flatpak, MCP, browser verification, team sync, third-party skill registry.

---

### 5.6 `v1.0` — Polished launch

**Outcome:** No new code in this milestone. Bug bash, polish, launch.

**Bug bash & QA**
- Two-week code freeze before launch. Only P0/P1 fixes during freeze.
- Manual QA pass across macOS (Intel + Apple Silicon), Ubuntu LTS, Fedora, Arch.
- Zero known P0 at launch; published list of known P1+ issues.

**Marketing & launch**
- Landing page (could share docs site or be separate).
- Demo video (3–5 min, screen recording with voiceover, one real task end-to-end — explicitly showing the pause-for-question moment, since that's the differentiator).
- Launch posts: README polish, blog post, HN, /r/programming, Twitter/X.
- Press kit: app screenshots, logo, one-paragraph description, longer description.
- Comparison guide: how this differs from Cursor, Continue.dev, raw Claude Code, Aider.

**Operational**
- `SECURITY.md` with disclosure contact + PGP key.
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` (standard Contributor Covenant).
- GitHub issue templates (bug, feature, question) and PR template.
- Versioning commitment in README: semver, what counts as breaking, deprecation policy.
- Support story: spell out whether issues / PRs are accepted, response-time expectations, what's out of scope.

## 6. Artifact taxonomy

A consolidated view of every artifact the system writes, who writes it, where it lives, and which milestone introduces it.

| Artifact | Writer | Worktree path | Durable path | First milestone |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `task-brief.md` | Intake (deterministic v0.1, agentic v0.2+) | `.aistudio/task-brief.md` | `.../tasks/{task_id}/artifacts/task-brief.md` | v0.1 |
| `CLAUDE.md` (managed section) | `WorktreeContextService` | `CLAUDE.md` (root) | — | v0.1 |
| `AGENTS.md` (managed section) | `WorktreeContextService` (Codex path) | `AGENTS.md` (root) | — | v0.2 |
| `spec.md` | `aistudio:intake` skill | `.aistudio/spec.md` | `.../artifacts/spec.md` | v0.2 |
| `implementation-plan.md` | `aistudio:intake` (same pass as spec) | `.aistudio/implementation-plan.md` | `.../artifacts/implementation-plan.md` | v0.2 |
| `test-plan.md` | `aistudio:intake` | `.aistudio/test-plan.md` | `.../artifacts/test-plan.md` | v0.2 |
| `constraints.md` | `aistudio:intake` | `.aistudio/constraints.md` | `.../artifacts/constraints.md` | v0.2 |
| `out-of-scope.md` | `aistudio:intake` | `.aistudio/out-of-scope.md` | `.../artifacts/out-of-scope.md` | v0.2 |
| `interview-transcript.md` | `aistudio:intake` | `.aistudio/interview-transcript.md` | `.../artifacts/interview-transcript.md` | v0.2 |
| `journal.md` | Agent (via `aistudio:journal-checkpoint`) | `.aistudio/journal.md` | `.../artifacts/journal.md` | v0.2 |
| `assumptions.md` | Agent (via `aistudio:assumption-log`) | `.aistudio/assumptions.md` | `.../artifacts/assumptions.md` | v0.2 |
| `questions.md` | Agent (via `aistudio:pause-for-question`); user appends answers | `.aistudio/questions.md` | `.../artifacts/questions.md` | v0.2 |
| `blockers.md` | Agent | `.aistudio/blockers.md` | `.../artifacts/blockers.md` | v0.2 |
| Terminal log | `ProcessRunner` | — | `.../artifacts/logs/run-{n}.log` | v0.1 |
| Git diff snapshot | `GitService` | — | `.../artifacts/diffs/diff-{n}.patch` | v0.1 |
| Verification logs | `VerificationService` | — | `.../artifacts/verifications/{cmd}-{n}.log` | v0.1 |
| PR evidence report | `aistudio:generate-evidence` (v0.2+); template in v0.1 | — | `.../artifacts/reports/pr-evidence.md` + PR body | v0.1 |

`.aistudio/` is added to the worktree's `.gitignore` by the harness. Reviewers see the artifact contents via the PR evidence report, not as files in the PR diff.

## 7. Skill catalog

Skills the harness ships and installs into the worktree on engine start. All built from scratch as first-class AI Software Studio components; the `Modeled on` column names the superpowers skill whose pattern informed the design, not a source of vendored content.

| Skill | Modeled on | Triggers / called by | Reads | Writes | Milestone |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `aistudio:intake` | `superpowers:brainstorming` | Task creation when intake is enabled | User answers, existing repo context | `spec.md`, `implementation-plan.md`, `test-plan.md`, `constraints.md`, `out-of-scope.md`, `interview-transcript.md` | v0.2 |
| `aistudio:write-plan` | `superpowers:writing-plans` | After intake (separable for issue-imported tasks) | `spec.md` | `implementation-plan.md` | v0.2 |
| `aistudio:tdd-cycle` | `superpowers:test-driven-development` | At each implementation step from `implementation-plan.md` | `implementation-plan.md`, `test-plan.md` | failing test commit → impl commit → refactor commit | v0.2 |
| `aistudio:journal-checkpoint` | — (native) | After each meaningful step | step context | appends to `journal.md` | v0.2 |
| `aistudio:pause-for-question` | — (native) | When agent hits ambiguity it can't resolve from artifacts | nothing | appends to `questions.md`, calls harness's `pause_with_pending_questions` | v0.2 |
| `aistudio:assumption-log` | — (native) | When agent decides on ambiguity without asking | decision context | appends to `assumptions.md` | v0.2 |
| `aistudio:verify-acceptance` | `superpowers:verification-before-completion` | Before marking task `ReviewReady` | `implementation-plan.md`, `test-plan.md`, project verification commands | verification outputs | v0.2 |
| `aistudio:generate-evidence` | — (native) | On task complete | all artifacts | PR evidence report | v0.2 |

**Skill packaging:**
- **v0.2:** plain Markdown skill bodies in `{worktree}/.aistudio/skills/`, loaded by `@`-import from `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`. Identical surface for both engines because no engine-native skill mechanism is used yet.
- **v0.3:** engine-agnostic skill manifest (name, description, body, optional triggers) with per-engine adapters that translate to each engine's native skill loading: Claude → `.claude/skills/`; Codex → its equivalent (TBD during early v0.2 spike, see open items).

**Skill discovery:** the bundled skill set is fixed in 1.0. Third-party skill registry is 1.1+.

## 8. Hook catalog

Events the Rust harness automatically triggers. Hooks are how the harness participates in the agentic flow without scripting every step.

| Hook | Triggered by | Harness action |
|---|---|---|
| `on-engine-start` | Task transitions to `Running` | Write `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`, install skills, drop artifacts into `.aistudio/`, start file watchers |
| `on-step-complete` | Skill `aistudio:journal-checkpoint` invoked | Update task progress indicator in UI |
| `on-question-appended` | File watcher on `questions.md` sees a new question | Transition task to `NeedsInput`, surface UI banner |
| `on-question-answered` | User submits an answer in the conversation panel | Append answer to `questions.md`, transition task back to `Running`, resume engine |
| `on-engine-halt` | Engine process exits | Collect `questions.md` + `blockers.md`, transition task accordingly |
| `on-task-complete` | Task transitions to `ReviewReady` | Invoke `aistudio:generate-evidence` to render the PR report |

## 9. Cross-cutting habits

Not separate tracks — built into every PR from the milestone they first apply.

- **Logging/tracing** (from `v0.1`): instrument new code as you write it; do not retrofit in `v0.3`.
- **Accessibility patterns** (from `v0.1`): keyboard nav, focus rings, ARIA on components when built. The `v0.9` audit closes gaps; it should not be the first time a11y is considered.
- **Performance measurement** (from `v0.5`): manual measurement during `v0.1`–`v0.3`; CI-enforced budgets land in `v0.9`.
- **Agent context comes from artifacts, not ad-hoc prompts** (from `v0.2`): when adding a new piece of context the agent needs, the right move is a new artifact + a `CLAUDE.md` `@`-import, not a longer prompt body. Prompts stay minimal; artifacts carry the load.

## 10. Risk register

| Risk | Milestone | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| `gh pr create` fails on first real use (auth, branch, base mismatch) | v0.1 | Surface stderr verbatim; "Copy PR report" stays as fallback |
| `WorktreeContextService` corrupts an existing project `CLAUDE.md` | v0.1 | Strict marker-based append; integration test that round-trips a non-trivial existing file |
| PTY edge cases differ between Claude Code and Codex | v0.2 | Build behind a flag; keep plain stdio path working as fallback |
| Crash recovery races with engine processes still alive | v0.2 | Scan-on-startup is read-only; only mark failed, never restart automatically; preserve `NeedsInput` tasks intact |
| Agent ignores `aistudio:pause-for-question` skill and guesses anyway | v0.2 | Reinforce in the `CLAUDE.md` rules block; integration test injects a deliberately ambiguous task and asserts the agent halted; user can still review and reject silently-guessed work |
| Hard TDD git-history verifier produces false positives | v0.2 | Per-task override with mandatory comment (logged as assumption); clear error message naming which commit violated the gate |
| Schema migration on existing user data corrupts state | v0.3 | Backup `app.db` before every migration; ship a `--restore-backup` flag |
| Skill loading change between v0.2 (`@`-import) and v0.3 (engine-native) breaks templates created in v0.2 | v0.3 | One-way template upgrade migration; ship a migration test |
| Homebrew cask + xattr quarantine workaround stops working on a new macOS version | v0.5 | Watch macOS beta channel; have an Apple Developer fallback plan documented even if not enrolled |
| Tauri auto-updater bricks Linux installs | v0.5 | Stage updates: first push to a `beta` channel, real users on `stable` |
| Sentry leaks sensitive data through a stack trace or artifact path | v0.9 | Audit `beforeSend` hook; integration test asserts no repo paths, no `spec.md` excerpts, no journal contents in test crash payloads |
| Bug bash uncovers a v0.1-era architectural issue | v1.0 | Two-week freeze starts late enough that a 1-week emergency fix doesn't blow the launch |

## 11. Sequencing assumption

Milestones are strictly linear. Only one feature milestone is in flight at any time.

The single carve-out is the CI/release pipeline, scaffolded in `v0.1` and matured continuously. This is a one-time setup, not a parallel track.

Docs site does not start until `v0.9` (deliberately late — the surface needs to stabilize first).

## 12. Open items (not blocking)

Deferred until the relevant milestone:

- Whether to ship product analytics (PostHog or similar) in addition to Sentry. Current decision: no analytics in 1.0; revisit at 1.1.
- Docs site domain (`docs.aisoftware.studio` requires DNS) vs GitHub Pages fallback. Decide during `v0.9`.
- App icon source (commission vs. self-design). Decide during `v0.5`.
- Whether the v1.0 landing page is part of the docs site or a separate marketing page. Decide during `v1.0`.
- Final Codex skill-loading mechanism (depends on what Codex CLI actually supports for project-instruction files at the time we wire it). Spike during early v0.2.
- Whether the agentic intake should run in the same worktree as the implementation, or a throwaway scratch worktree. Decide during early v0.2 (recommend: same worktree, so the interview transcript is preserved with the task).

## 13. Next step

Generate the `v0.1` implementation plan via the `writing-plans` skill, into `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-18-v0.1-personal-mvp-plan.md` (gitignored). Subsequent milestone plans are generated on demand as each milestone approaches — not all up front, to avoid plans going stale before they're executed.
